caption: Kampong Glam heritage district viewed from above, showing dense conservation shophouses with orange-red tile roofs arranged along a street grid. The Sultan Mosque with its distinctive dome and surrounding green grounds is visible in the upper right — one of Singapore's most important religious landmarks. The heritage shophouse terraces create a uniform roofscape pattern broken by the mosque's grounds and a few taller modern buildings at the edges.

analysis.notable: Kampong Glam's Malay-Arab heritage quarter with the Sultan Mosque dome visible among conservation shophouse rooftops — Singapore's historic Muslim cultural precinct gazetted as a national monument.
